7946.built_by: Devonport Dockyard (Plymouth, U.K.) : Vickers Armstrong (Newcastle-on-Tyne, U.K.)
7946.history: The Royal Navy light-cruiser HMS Leander was loaned to the New Zealand Division from 30 April 1937 until 8 May 1944. At this time she was repairing and refitting at Boston in the USA. HMS Leander returned to service on 27 August 1945. Decommissioned by the Royal Navy in February 1948. Sold to be broken up for scrap on 15 December 1949. Scrapped at Blyth on 15 January 1950.
commanders: Cdr. Harold Brittain Clifford Gill, RN (Dec 1944 - 24 Feb 1945)
Capt. Robert Jocelyn Oliver Otway-Ruthven, DSO, RN (24 Feb 1945 - mid 1947)

7947.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.): Wallsend
7947.history: Transferred to the Royal New Zealand Navy on 22 September 1943. Returned to the Royal Navy on 27 March 1946. Decommissioned in December 1960. Scrapped by Ward, Inverkeithing arriving on 5 December 1968.
commanders: Capt. Newton James Wallop William-Powlett, DSC, RN (22 Sep 1943 - 28 Apr 1945)
Capt. Ralph Alan Bevan Edwards, RN (28 Apr 1945 - ca. Oct 45)

7948.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.)
7948.history: Decommissioned in 1957. Scrapped at Dalmuir by Arnott Young, arriving on 8 August 1960.
commanders: Capt. William Geoffrey Arthur Robson, DSO, DSC, RN (Jun 1945 - Apr 1947)

7949.history: Construction suspended in January 1945. Cancelled in 1946.

7950.history: Originally named HMS Minotaur but transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy and named HMCS Ontario upon completion. Never served in the Royal Navy. Decommissioned on on 15 October 1958. She arrived at Osaka, Japan for breaking up on 19 November 1960.

7951.built_by: Western Pipe & Steel Co. (San Francisco, California, U.S.A.)
7951.history: Never commissioned into the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 5 January 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 26 February 1946. Sold into merchantile service on 28 October 1946 being renamed Castel Forte. Renamed Fairsky in 1958. Fairsky hit a submerged wreck on 23 June 1977 and was beached to prevent sinking. Refloated on 29 June 1977. Conversion to floating hotel Philippine Tourist started in 1978. Destroyed by a fire on 3 November 1979 and scrapped at Hong Kong starting on 24 May 1980.

7952.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 12 February 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 28 March 1946. Sold to be broken up for scrap on 14 May 1946.

7953.history: Never commissioned in the United states Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 29 December 1945. Stricken by the United States Navy on 26 February 1946. Sold into merchantile service on 17 January 1947 being renamed Almdijk. Sold to be broken up for scrap in October 1965. Scrapped in Spain.

7954.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 12 May 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 3 July 1946. Sold into merchantile service on 20 December 1946 being renamed Aagtekerk.

7955.built_by: Western Pipe & Steel Co. (San Francisco, California, U.S.A.)
7955.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 11 December 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 28 January 1947. Sold into merchantile service being renamed Sydney. In 1968 Sydney was renamed Roma, and later that year resold to become the Galaxy Queen. Sometime after this date she changed owners twice, becoming Lady Dina in 1972 and then Cariba II in 1973. In September 1975 she was sold to be broken up for scrap at La Spezia, Italy.

7956.built_by: Western Pipe & Steel Co. (San Francisco, California, U.S.A.)
7956.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 29 December 1945. Stricken by the United States Navy on 20 March 1946. Sold into merchantile service being renamed Riouw. Renamed Lobito in 1968. Scrapped in Taiwan in September 1975.

7957.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 12 February 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 28 March 1946. Sold to be broken up for scrap in May 1946.

7958.built_by: Western Pipe & Steel Co. (San Francisco, California, U.S.A.)
7958.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 12 February 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 28 March 1946. Sold to be broken up for scrap in June 1946.

7959.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 27 February 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 12 April 1946. Sold into merchantile service being renamed Robin Mowbray. Scrapped in Taiwan in January 1970.

7960.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 6 April 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 19 June 1946. Sold into merchantile service being renamed Alblasserdijk. Sold and renamed Irene Valmas. Scrapped in Spain in January 1971.

7961.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 4 February 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 28 March 1946. Sold to be broken up for scrap on 21 June 1946.

7962.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 26 January 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 19 July 1946. Sold into merchantile service being renamed Rempang. Renamed Daphne in 1968. Scrapped in Spain in January 1975.

7963.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 17 July 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 25 September 1946. Sold into merchantile service in 1948 being renamed Lancero. Renamed President Osmena in 1965. Scrapped in Taiwan in 1972.

7964.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Altough she was Canadian manned she was not commissioned into the Royal Canadian Navy. HMS Nabob (Capt. H.N. Lay) was torpedoed and damaged in the Arctic off the North Cape in position 71.42N, 19.11E by the German submarine U-354. After the badly damaged ship managed to limp to Britain she was judged not worth repairing. She was towed to Rosyth, beached and abandoned. Decommissioned on 30 Sepember 1944 she was retained in nominal reserve, but was cannibalized for spare parts for sisterships. On 16 March 1946, the vessel was returned to the US Navy and stricken. Sold for scrap in March 1947 in the Netherlands. However, she was resold to the Norddeutscher Lloyd, converted to the German motor merchant Nabob until 1952 and used as a civilian training vessel for the post war German merchant service. Renamed Glory in 1968 and registered in Panama. In December 1977 she was sold for scrap in Taiwan.

7965.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 2 April 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 21 May 1946. Sold into merchantile service in 1947 being renamed Rhodesia Star In 1967 she was renamed Hong Kong Knight. Scrapped in Taiwan in February 1974.

7966.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 6 December 1945. Stricken by the United States Navy on 7 February 1946. Sold into merchantile service on 20 June 1947 being renamed Salta. Salta was scrapped at Buenos Aires in June 1966.

7967.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 13 December 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 7 February 1947. Sold into merchantile service being renamed Almkerk. Sold in 1969 and renamed Pacific Reliance. Scrapped in Taiwan in February 1974.

7968.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 13 December 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 7 February 1947. Sold into merchantile service on 7 July 1947 being renamed Drente. Renamed Lambros in 1966. Renamed Ulysses in 1969. Scrapped in Taiwan in June 1975.

7969.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 21 November 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 22 January 1947. Sold into merchantile in 1948 being renamed Friesland. Renamed Pacific Breeze in 1967. Scrapped in Taiwan in May 1974.

7970.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 3 March 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 12 April 1946. Sold into merchantile service as Greystoke Castle. Renamed Gallic in 1954. Renamed Berinnes in 1959. Scrapped in Taiwan in November 1973.

7971.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. HMS Thane was torpedoed by the German submarine U-1172 off the Clyde Light Vessel, but survived and was towed to Greenock by HMS Loring, deemed beyond economical repair, declared a Constructive Total Loss and put on reserve March 1945. Returned to the United States Navy on 15 December 1945. Stricken for disposal. Sold to be broken up for scrap. Scrapped at Faslane in 1946.

7972.history: Never commissioned into the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 31 October 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 22 Janaury 1947. Sold into merchantile service on 29 July 1947 being renamed Roebiah. Renamed President Marcos in 1967. Scrapped in Taiwan in 1972.

7973.history: Never commissioned into the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 29 January 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy 20 March 1946. Sold to be broken up for scrap on 31 May 1946.

7974.history: Never commissioned into the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 3 March 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 12 April 1946. Sold into merchantile service on 30 January 1947 neing renamed as Coracero. Renamed President Macapagal in 1965. Scrapped in Taiwan in May 1972.

7975.history: Never commissioned into the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 6 April 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 21 May 1946. Sold into merchantile service being renamed Artillero. Renamed President Garcia in 1965. Wrecked off the island of Guernsey in July 1967. Scrapped at Hamburg in November 1967.

7976.history: Never commissioned into the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Altough she was Canadian manned she was not commissioned into the Royal Canadian Navy. Returned to the United States Navy on 16 January 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 12 March 1946. Sold into merchantile service being renamed Muncaster Castle. Renamed Bardic in 1954. Renamed Ben Nevis in 1959. Scrapped in Taiwan in June 1973.

7977.history: Never commissioned in the United States Navy. Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 20 May 1946. Stricken by the United States Navy on 2 July 1946. Sold into merchantile service being renamed South Africa Star. Scrapped in Japan in May 1967.

7978.history: Loaned to the Canadian Navy upon completion as HMCS Warrior. Decommissioned by the Royal Canadian Navy on 23 March 1948 and returned to the Royal Navy. Commissioned by the Royal Navy in November 1948 and mainly used as a trials carrier. Decommissioned to reserve in September 1949 but recommissioned in June 1950 for service as a troop transport to Korea. Refitted at Devonport Dockyard from March 1952 until 8 September 1953. Refitted further from 14 December 1954 until 21 August 1956. Decommissioned in February 1958. Sold to Argentina on 4 July 1958. Commissioned into the Argentine Navy on 8 July 1959 as Independencia. Decommissioned into reserve 1970. Sold to be broken up for scrap on 17 March 1971.

7979.history: Never commissioned in the Royal Navy. Transferred to the Royal Indian Navy before completion.

Stricken on 28 April 1945. Sold to be broken up for scrap on 11 March 1946.
